en: "This is the law of the guilt offering. It is most holy.
zh: “贖愆祭的律例是這樣：這祭是至聖的。

en: In the place where they kill the burnt offering they shall kill the guilt offering, and its blood shall be thrown against the sides of the altar.
zh: 人在甚麼地方宰殺燔祭牲，就要在那裡宰殺贖愆祭牲，牠的血要潑在祭壇的四周。

en: And all its fat shall be offered, the fat tail, the fat that covers the entrails,
zh: 祭司要把祭牲的一切脂肪都獻上，就是肥尾巴和遮蓋內臟的脂肪，

en: the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins, and the long lobe of the liver that he shall remove with the kidneys.
zh: 以及兩個腎、腎上近腰旁的脂肪和腎旁的肝葉，一起取下。

en: The priest shall burn them on the altar as a food offering to the LORD; it is a guilt offering.
zh: 然後把這些焚燒在祭壇上，作獻給耶和華的火祭，這是贖愆祭。

en: Every male among the priests may eat of it. It shall be eaten in a holy place. It is most holy.
zh: 祭司家中的男丁，都可以吃祭牲的肉，要在聖潔的地方吃；這是至聖的。

en: The guilt offering is just like the sin offering; there is one law for them. The priest who makes atonement with it shall have it.
zh: 贖罪祭怎樣，贖愆祭也怎樣；兩個祭的律例相同；祭物要歸主持贖罪的祭司，

en: And the priest who offers any man's burnt offering shall have for himself the skin of the burnt offering that he has offered.
zh: 祭司替人獻燔祭，祭牲的皮要歸獻燔祭的祭司。

en: And every grain offering baked in the oven and all that is prepared on a pan or a griddle shall belong to the priest who offers it.
zh: 一切在爐裡烤的，或在淺鍋裡或在煎盤上做的素祭，都要歸給那獻祭的祭司。

en: And every grain offering, mixed with oil or dry, shall be shared equally among all the sons of Aaron.
zh: 一切用油調和，或是乾的素祭，都要歸給亞倫所有的子孫，大家均分。

en: "And this is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings that one may offer to the LORD.
zh: “獻給耶和華的平安祭的律例是這樣：

en: If he offers it for a thanksgiving, then he shall offer with the thanksgiving sacrifice unleavened loaves mixed with oil, unleavened wafers smeared with oil, and loaves of fine flour well mixed with oil.
zh: 人若是為感恩獻祭，就要用調油的無酵餅和抹了油的無酵薄餅，以及用油調和了細麵做的餅，與感恩祭一同獻上。

en: With the sacrifice of his peace offerings for thanksgiving he shall bring his offering with loaves of leavened bread.
zh: 他要把他的供物，連同有酵的餅，跟那為感恩而獻的平安祭，一起獻上。

en: And from it he shall offer one loaf from each offering, as a gift to the LORD. It shall belong to the priest who throws the blood of the peace offerings.
zh: 他要從各物中，取出一個餅作為舉祭，獻給耶和華，歸給那潑平安祭牲之血的祭司。

en: And the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offerings for thanksgiving shall be eaten on the day of his offering. He shall not leave any of it until the morning.
zh: “為感恩而獻的平安祭的肉，只能在獻祭那日吃，一點也不可留到早晨。

en: But if the sacrifice of his offering is a vow offering or a freewill offering, it shall be eaten on the day that he offers his sacrifice, and on the next day what remains of it shall be eaten.
zh: 他獻的供物若是還願祭，或是甘心祭，獻祭那日可以吃；剩下的，第二天也可以吃。

en: But what remains of the flesh of the sacrifice on the third day shall be burned up with fire.
zh: 還剩下的祭肉，到了第三天，就要用火燒掉。

en: If any of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offering is eaten on the third day, he who offers it shall not be accepted, neither shall it be credited to him. It is tainted, and he who eats of it shall bear his iniquity.
zh: 如果在第三天吃平安祭的肉，必不蒙悅納，他獻上的也不再算為祭，因為祭肉已經成為不潔；吃這肉的人，必擔當自己的罪責。

en: "Flesh that touches any unclean thing shall not be eaten. It shall be burned up with fire. All who are clean may eat flesh,
zh: “祭肉若是接觸了任何不潔，就不可以吃，要用火燒掉。潔淨的人才可以吃祭肉；

en: but the person who eats of the flesh of the sacrifice of the LORD's peace offerings while an uncleanness is on him, that person shall be cut off from his people.
zh: 身上不潔淨的人吃了獻與耶和華的平安祭的祭肉，那人必從自己的族人中被剪除。

en: And if anyone touches an unclean thing, whether human uncleanness or an unclean beast or any unclean detestable creature, and then eats some flesh from the sacrifice of the LORD's peace offerings, that person shall be cut off from his people."
zh: 人若是摸了任何不潔，或是人的污穢，或是牲畜的污穢，或是任何不潔淨的可憎之物，然後吃獻給耶和華的平安祭的肉，那人必從自己的族人中被剪除。”

en: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：

en: "Speak to the people of Israel, saying, You shall eat no fat, of ox or sheep or goat.
zh: “你要告訴以色列人說：所有公牛、綿羊或是山羊的脂肪，你們都不可吃。

en: The fat of an animal that dies of itself and the fat of one that is torn by beasts may be put to any other use, but on no account shall you eat it.
zh: 自死的或被野獸撕裂的動物的脂肪，可以作別的用途，只是你們決不可吃。

en: For every person who eats of the fat of an animal of which a food offering may be made to the LORD shall be cut off from his people.
zh: 因為無論何人吃了獻給耶和華為火祭的牲畜的脂肪，那吃的人就必從自己的族人中被剪除。

en: Moreover, you shall eat no blood whatever, whether of fowl or of animal, in any of your dwelling places.
zh: 在你們的一切住處，任何血，無論是飛鳥或是牲畜的，你們都不可吃。

en: Whoever eats any blood, that person shall be cut off from his people."
zh: 無論誰吃了甚麼血，那人必從自己的族人中被剪除。”

en: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：

en: "Speak to the people of Israel, saying, Whoever offers the sacrifice of his peace offerings to the LORD shall bring his offering to the LORD from the sacrifice of his peace offerings.
zh: “你要告訴以色列人：向耶和華獻平安祭的，必須從他的平安祭中取出奉給耶和華的供物。

en: His own hands shall bring the LORD's food offerings. He shall bring the fat with the breast, that the breast may be waved as a wave offering before the LORD.
zh: 親手把獻給耶和華的火祭帶來，就是把脂肪連胸帶來，在耶和華面前搖一搖，作為搖祭。

en: The priest shall burn the fat on the altar, but the breast shall be for Aaron and his sons.
zh: 祭司要把脂肪焚燒在祭壇上，胸歸給亞倫和他的子孫。

en: And the right thigh you shall give to the priest as a contribution from the sacrifice of your peace offerings.
zh: 你們要從平安祭牲中，拿右後腿來作舉祭，奉給祭司。

en: Whoever among the sons of Aaron offers the blood of the peace offerings and the fat shall have the right thigh for a portion.
zh: 亞倫的子孫中，誰獻平安祭牲的血和脂肪，右後腿就歸給他，是他的分。

en: For the breast that is waved and the thigh that is contributed I have taken from the people of Israel, out of the sacrifices of their peace offerings, and have given them to Aaron the priest and to his sons, as a perpetual due from the people of Israel.
zh: 因為我從以色列人的平安祭中，取出了這搖祭的胸和舉祭的後腿，給亞倫祭司和他的子孫，成為以色列人中永遠的律例。

en: This is the portion of Aaron and of his sons from the LORD's food offerings, from the day they were presented to serve as priests of the LORD.
zh: “這是亞倫和他子孫，從奉獻他們作耶和華的祭司那日開始，受膏立應得的分。

en: The LORD commanded this to be given them by the people of Israel, from the day that he anointed them. It is a perpetual due throughout their generations."
zh: 就是耶和華膏立他們的時候，命令以色列人給他們的。這是世世代代永遠的律例。”

en: This is the law of the burnt offering, of the grain offering, of the sin offering, of the guilt offering, of the ordination offering, and of the peace offering,
zh: 以上關於燔祭、素祭、贖罪祭、贖愆祭、承受聖職祭和平安祭的律例，

en: which the LORD commanded Moses on Mount Sinai, on the day that he commanded the people of Israel to bring their offerings to the LORD, in the wilderness of Sinai.
zh: 都是耶和華在西奈山吩咐摩西的，就是他在西奈的曠野吩咐以色列人，把他們的供物獻給耶和華的時候所說的。
